content.text: The Azure Portal (portal.azure.com) is the primary web-based graphical interface for managing Azure resources. It runs entirely in the browser, requires no installation, and provides access to every Azure service. Understanding its layout lets you navigate directly to what you need and avoid clicking through unrelated menus.

attrs.alt: Azure Portal UI regions: top bar, left sidebar, main blade area, command bar, and Cloud Shell

content.text: The Portal's core UI metaphor is the blade — a panel that slides in from the right when you select a resource, service, or setting. Blades stack horizontally, and the breadcrumb trail at the top reflects the current path. You can pin any blade width, scroll horizontally through stacked blades, or press the back button to close the top blade.

Blade = a full panel. Side panel = the narrower context pane that opens alongside a blade without replacing it. Knowing this distinction prevents confusion when documentation refers to 'the blade' vs 'the context pane'.
Resource overview blade: essentials (name, resource group, location, subscription, tags), status, metrics, and the settings navigation on the left
Settings sub-blades: child blades opened from the resource's left-hand menu (e.g., Networking, Access control, Locks)
Context pane: narrow panel that appears to the right of the main blade for quick edits without full navigation
Full-screen mode: maximize a blade with the diagonal arrows icon for complex views like the metrics chart editor
The top bar is always visible and provides cross-resource functionality regardless of which blade is open.
Search resources, services, documentation, Marketplace items by name or type
Return to the portal home page or your pinned dashboard
Launch an embedded Bash or PowerShell terminal authenticated to your subscriptions
Switch which Azure AD tenant and subscriptions the portal displays
View recent operations: deployments, role assignments, policy evaluations
Change portal language, theme (light/dark/high-contrast), startup page, and timeout
Switch directories, sign in as a different user, view role assignments, sign out

The collapsible sidebar gives quick access to favourite services and home navigation. You can customise which services appear under 'Favourites' — right-click any service in All Services and choose 'Add to favourites'.
+ Create a resource: shortcut to the Marketplace, equivalent to selecting 'Create' inside any service blade
Home: portal home with recent resources, recent services, and quick-start tutorials
Dashboard: the customisable pinboard of tiles; multiple dashboards supported
All services: filterable catalogue of every Azure service organised by category
Favourites: pinned services for fast access (editable, persists per account across devices)

# To add a service to Favourites:
1. Click "All services" in the left sidebar
2. Search for the service (e.g., "Key vaults")
3. Click the star (☆) next to the service name
4. The service now appears in the Favourites section

# To reorder Favourites:
1. Click the settings (gear) icon in the top bar
2. Select "Portal settings > Appearance + startup views"
3. Under "Favorites" drag to reorder

The portal home page and a dashboard are different. The home page is the fixed landing page showing recently visited resources, recently used services, and Azure news. A dashboard is a pinboard of customisable tiles that you build yourself.
Recent resources, recent services, Azure news (auto-generated)
Tiles you choose: metrics charts, resource lists, links, markdown, clock
Fully customisable: add, move, resize, delete tiles
Multiple; shareable with other users via RBAC
Can set as startup page in portal settings
Yes: 'Download > Download as JSON' for backup or ARM deployment
The global search bar (Ctrl + /) is the fastest way to navigate. It searches across resource names, resource types, documentation, Marketplace, and management plane commands simultaneously.

# Examples of what global search finds:
Type "vnet-prod"      → shows the specific virtual network resource
Type "virtual"        → shows all virtual network resources + the Virtual Machines service
Type "rg-"            → lists resource groups starting with "rg-"
Type "az vm"          → surfaces Azure CLI documentation pages
Type "create storage" → Marketplace items and service documentation

# Keyboard shortcuts inside search results:
Enter         → navigate to the top result
Arrow keys    → move through results
Esc           → close the search panel
Ctrl+/        → focus the search box from anywhere in the portal
Inside a resource group or subscription blade, an additional search/filter bar appears at the top of the resource list. This filters only within that scope — it is separate from the global search.

# Resource-scoped filter options (inside resource group blade):
Filter by name    → type partial resource name
Filter by type    → e.g., "Virtual machine"
Filter by location → e.g., "East US"
Filter by tag     → key=value pair

Many Azure subscriptions belonging to different Azure AD tenants may be associated with your account. The directory and subscription filter at the top controls which subscriptions' resources appear throughout the portal.

# To switch the active directory or filter subscriptions:
1. Click the filter icon in the top bar (looks like a funnel with a portal symbol)
2. Under "Directories", select the Azure AD tenant
3. Under "Subscriptions", tick/untick individual subscriptions
4. Click "Apply"

# If a resource is not visible in the portal, first check:
- Is the correct directory selected?
- Is the correct subscription selected in the filter?
- Do you have at least Reader role on the resource?

Azure Cloud Shell (Ctrl + `) is a browser-based terminal available directly in the portal. It is pre-authenticated to your subscriptions with no login required, has all Azure CLI, PowerShell Az, kubectl, Terraform, and other tools pre-installed, and persists files in a storage account across sessions.

# Cloud Shell launches automatically authenticated; run commands immediately:
az account show        # Shows the currently selected subscription
az account list        # Lists all subscriptions you have access to

# Switch subscriptions within Cloud Shell
az account set --subscription "sub-production"

# Upload files using the upload button (toolbar) then reference them
az deployment group create \
  --resource-group rg-prod \
  --template-file main.bicep        # file uploaded via Cloud Shell toolbar

Cloud Shell sessions time out after 20 minutes of inactivity. Files saved to the clouddrive mount (/home/<user>/clouddrive) persist, but files saved elsewhere in the container are lost on timeout or restart.

The bell icon in the top bar shows recent portal operations (last ~100 operations). For a full audit trail, use Monitor > Activity Log which retains 90 days of events and supports export to Log Analytics or a storage account.

# Access the same data via CLI (no portal required)
az monitor activity-log list \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --start-time 2024-01-01 \
  --end-time 2024-01-08 \
  --max-events 50 \
  -o table

# Filter by operation
az monitor activity-log list \
  --resource-group rg-production \
  --caller "user@contoso.com" \
  --status "Failed" \
  -o table
Portal settings (gear icon) let each user customise their experience without affecting other users. Settings are stored per Microsoft account and synchronise across devices.
Dark for low-light; High Contrast for accessibility
Pin your most-used dashboard as the landing page
Set shorter timeout on shared machines
Dock the left sidebar to keep it always expanded
The portal has keyboard shortcuts for common actions. Press ? in the portal to view the full list.

Ctrl + /        Global search
Ctrl + `        Open / close Cloud Shell
G + H           Go to Home
G + D           Go to Dashboard
G + A           Go to All services
G + N           Open the 'Create a resource' page
G + F           Open Favourites
Shift + Space   Maximise / restore the current blade
Resource group or resource not visible
Click directory filter, select correct tenant and subscription
'You do not have access' error on a resource
No role assignment for your account on that scope
Ask an Owner to grant you at minimum the Reader role
Portal is very slow or times out
Browser extensions interfering; too many open blades
Open portal in a private/incognito window; close unused blades
Cloud Shell prompts for storage account setup
First-time use; Cloud Shell needs a storage account
Accept the default or pick existing storage account and file share
Can't find a service in the sidebar
Search globally or go to All Services and star the service
Close the browser tab, open portal.azure.com in a new tab
The portal offers several navigation paths to the same resource. Understanding all of them prevents the frustration of searching when you already know the resource name or type.
Every resource in Azure has a permanent URL in the portal. You can navigate directly by constructing or bookmarking these URLs.

# Resource group URL pattern
https://portal.azure.com/#@<tenant-id>/resource/subscriptions/<sub-id>/resourceGroups/<rg-name>/overview

# Virtual machine URL pattern
https://portal.azure.com/#@<tenant-id>/resource/subscriptions/<sub-id>/resourceGroups/<rg-name>/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/<vm-name>/overview

# Storage account URL pattern
https://portal.azure.com/#@<tenant-id>/resource/subscriptions/<sub-id>/resourceGroups/<rg-name>/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/<account-name>/overview

# Service catalogue URL (direct link to Virtual Machines service)
https://portal.azure.com/#view/HubsExtension/BrowseResource/resourceType/Microsoft.Compute~2FvirtualMachines

Azure Resource Graph is accessible directly in the portal via the 'Resource Graph Explorer' service. It runs Kusto Query Language (KQL) queries across your entire Azure estate without opening individual blades.

// Find all virtual machines and their power state
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines"
| extend powerState = properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.displayStatus
| project name, resourceGroup, location, powerState, subscriptionId
| order by name asc

// Find resources without a required tag
Resources
| where tags !contains "Environment"
| project name, type, resourceGroup
| order by type asc

// Count resources by type across all subscriptions
Resources
| summarize count() by type
| order by count_ desc
| take 20

The portal 'Create' wizard is the guided path for provisioning a new resource. Every service provides a multi-tab wizard with Basics, Networking, Security, Tags, and Review + create tabs. The Review + create tab always shows the ARM template that the portal will submit — you can download it before creating the resource.
Downloading the ARM Template Before Deploying

# Portal workflow to export the ARM template:
1. Complete all tabs in the Create wizard
2. Click "Review + create"
3. Click "Download a template for automation" (link below the validation summary)
4. In the template editor blade, click "Download" to get a .zip with:
   - template.json (ARM template)
   - parameters.json (with parameter values from the wizard)
5. Optionally click "Deploy" to save the template to the Template Library

# You can redeploy the same configuration later:
az deployment group create \
  --resource-group rg-prod \
  --template-file template.json \
  --parameters @parameters.json
The portal wizard maps directly to CLI flags. Here is the equivalent of creating a storage account through the portal wizard using the CLI.

# Equivalent of the portal 'Create storage account' wizard:
az storage account create \
  --name mystorageaccount \
  --resource-group rg-prod \
  --location eastus \
  --sku Standard_LRS \
  --kind StorageV2 \
  --access-tier Hot \
  --https-only true \
  --min-tls-version TLS1_2 \
  --allow-blob-public-access false \
  --default-action Deny \
  --tags Environment=Production CostCentre=CC-001

# Check the result
az storage account show \
  --name mystorageaccount \
  --resource-group rg-prod \
  -o table

Any blade or chart in the portal can be pinned to a dashboard using the push-pin icon in the top-right corner of most portal elements. This builds a custom monitoring and navigation hub without opening multiple blades.
Resource overview tiles: shows resource name, status, and a link to the blade
Metrics charts: live-updating charts for CPU, memory, network, or custom metrics
Resource group tiles: shows all resources in a group
Query results from Log Analytics or Resource Graph Explorer
Markdown tiles: custom text, links, or HTML snippets for team documentation
Clock and time zone tiles for on-call dashboards

# To share a dashboard with other users:
1. Open the dashboard to share
2. Click "Share" in the command bar
3. Publishing to an Azure subscription makes it a resource (Microsoft.Portal/dashboards)
4. Assign read access via Azure RBAC: role "Reader" scoped to the dashboard resource
5. Recipients see the shared dashboard in their portal under "Shared dashboards"

# To export and import a dashboard as JSON (backup or copy to another tenant):
1. Open the dashboard
2. Click the kebab menu (...) > "Download"
3. Save the JSON file
4. On another account/tenant: Dashboard > "Upload"
5. Or deploy via ARM:
az resource create \
  --resource-type Microsoft.Portal/dashboards \
  --resource-group rg-shared \
  --location eastus \
  --properties @dashboard.json \
  --name "My Shared Dashboard"
Portal vs CLI vs REST: Choosing the Right Tool

Every portal action submits an ARM REST API call under the hood. The portal is best for discovery, inspection, and one-off tasks. CLI and REST are best for automation, repeatability, and CI/CD pipelines.
Bookmarking these portal URLs gives instant access to high-frequency management tasks without navigating through the sidebar.

# Core navigation
https://portal.azure.com/                         # Home page
https://portal.azure.com/#home                    # Home (explicit)
https://portal.azure.com/#dashboard               # Last-used dashboard
https://portal.azure.com/#allservices             # All services catalogue

# Cost management
https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_CostManagement/Menu/~/overview
https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_CostManagement/Menu/~/budgets

# Security
https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Security/SecurityMenuBlade/~/0

# Monitoring
https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Monitoring/AzureMonitoringBrowseBlade/~/overview
https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Monitoring_Logs/LogsBlade

# Resource Graph Explorer
https://portal.azure.com/#view/HubsExtension/ArgQueryBlade

# Subscriptions list
https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Billing/SubscriptionsBlade

# Azure Active Directory / Entra ID
https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_IAM/ActiveDirectoryMenuBlade/~/overview

The Azure Portal supports keyboard navigation throughout (Tab, Enter, Escape, arrow keys), high-contrast themes, and screen readers. Language and regional format settings are per-account in portal settings.

# High Contrast mode:
1. Settings (gear) > Appearance > Theme: High Contrast
2. Or press Alt+Shift+PrintScreen (Windows system high-contrast toggle)

# Full keyboard navigation:
Tab / Shift+Tab    Move between interactive elements
Enter              Activate focused button or link
Escape             Close the current blade or dialog
F6                 Move focus between main sections of the portal
Arrow keys         Navigate within lists and menus

# Language change:
Settings > Language + region > Language: select locale
Note: the portal UI language does not affect the Azure API or resource metadata

The Azure Portal is a blade-based web UI at portal.azure.com that provides access to every Azure service through a customisable sidebar, a global search bar, and context-sensitive command bars. The top navigation bar holds the directory/subscription filter, Cloud Shell, notifications, and portal settings. The left sidebar is customisable with Favourites. The main working area uses stacked blades to navigate resources. Cloud Shell (Ctrl+`) gives a pre-authenticated terminal directly in the browser. For automation, every portal action has a CLI or REST API equivalent — use the portal for discovery and investigation, and switch to CLI/ARM/Bicep for repeatable deployments.
